title: Approval Bottleneck
question: Why has execution speed slowed dramatically despite increased investment in people and technology?
context: Benchmark designed to test whether Discovery identifies decision latency and centralized governance as the dominant organizational mechanisms.

evidence.title: Executive Interviews
evidence.content: Department leaders report that nearly every significant operational decision requires executive approval before work can proceed.

evidence.title: Operations Review
evidence.content: The average time from identifying a production issue to approving corrective action has increased from 18 hours to 5.6 days.

evidence.title: Engineering Retrospective
evidence.content: Engineering teams frequently finish technical designs but wait several days for leadership approval before implementation can begin.

evidence.title: Employee Survey
evidence.content: 74% of managers report uncertainty about their authority to make routine operational decisions.

evidence.title: Procurement Audit
evidence.content: Purchasing requests over $2,500 require approvals from three management levels before orders can be placed.

evidence.title: Project Postmortem
evidence.content: Three major customer projects missed delivery dates because approval requests remained unresolved during executive travel.

evidence.title: Leadership Meeting
evidence.content: Executives expressed concern that decentralizing decisions could reduce quality and increase organizational risk.

evidence.title: Customer Success Report
evidence.content: Customer issues often remain unresolved while teams wait for authorization to provide refunds or implement corrective actions.

evidence.title: Continuous Improvement Program
evidence.content: Employees submitted more than 200 improvement suggestions, but fewer than 10 percent were implemented because they required senior leadership approval.

evidence.title: Organizational Observation
evidence.content: Departments execute effectively once work begins, but progress repeatedly pauses while awaiting cross-functional approvals.
